We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the following department heads to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No.  2015-008:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to:
Update the road inventory of the Municipality; and
Provide the Municipal Accountant and the Acting General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for every road project;
Require the Municipal Accountant to keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network;
Direct the General Services Officer to:
Maintain the Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and their components; and
Prepare the Report on Local Road Network; and
Direct the Inventory Committee to:
Prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Local Road Network (RPCLRN).

We recommended that Management take advantage of the use of ATM payroll system in the payment of wages, instead of manual payments through cash advances, to address the risk of possible loss of funds, save man-hours and streamline the payment process.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
The HRMO, Municipal Accountant, and the Cashier in charge of loan approvals to expedite the reconciliation of records with the GSIS and update the service records to enable full remittance of contributions to the GSIS;
The Municipal Accountant to trace the details of prior years’ balances by requesting the latest statements of account from the concerned national agencies; and
The Municipal Accountant to identify the causes of non-remittance for the remaining balance of unremitted contributions, and moving forward, to ensure that all contributions are remitted in full to the GSIS and Pag-IBIG within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Management require all job orders and casual employees to prepare and submit Accomplishment Report signed and verified by the immediate supervisors before processing claims for payment in compliance with Item 4.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended, and the Management acknowledged and committed to comply with the following:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to draw a check to facilitate the transfer of the unexpended/unobligated balance of the current year appropriation of the LDRRMF from the GF to the STF at the year-end; and
Direct the Municipal Account to ensure the transfer of the unexpended and unobligated balance of LDRRF as of year-end for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Acting Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant, and DRRM Officer to refrain from using the DRRMF for programs/projects/activities (PPAs) which are administrative in nature and not directly related to pre-disaster preparedness, mitigation, response and rescue operations to ensure that it is utilized solely for approved DRRM-related PPAs;
Direct the BAC and/or MDRRMO to provide a valid justification for why the procurement of food, venue, and accommodation was not conducted through public bidding, as required under RA No. 9184, but was instead split into two transactions and procured through the small value procurement method;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the procurement and consumption of goods are properly recorded under the appropriate inventory accounts and to recognize expenses only upon submission of the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI); and
Adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR in all stages of the procurement of goods to achieve transparency, efficiency, and economy in the procurement process.

We recommended, and the Management acknowledged and committed to comply with the following recommendations:
Require the concerned officials to submit the Gender and Development (GAD) Plan and Budget (GPB) and GAD Accomplishment Report (AR) within the prescribed period to facilitate the timely evaluation and validation of the GAD PPAs; and
Establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System through the issuance of an executive order to ensure that GAD activities are properly monitored and that gender-related initiatives truly benefit the community.

We recommended, and the Management acknowledged and committed to comply with the following:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit quarterly and annual reports using the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 (Annex B) on SEF utilization to the DepEd, copy furnished the Local Sanggunian concerned and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG;

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to establish the RPT Receivable/SET Receivable based on the duly Certified List of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year, the same must be reconciled with the records of the Municipal Treasurer at year-end; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to provide an updated Certified List of Real Property Tax Delinquencies as the basis for the recording of receivables in the books and enforce the collection of unpaid delinquent taxes through tax campaigns.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a.	Send confirmation letters from the creditors to validate the existence of the payables. If non-existent, revert the payables that have remained outstanding for a period of two (2) years or more; and
b. Effect the necessary adjustments and furnish the Office of the Auditor with a copy of the journal entry voucher.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require all department heads to meticulously and judiciously prepare and submit their respective Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) in consonance with the budget to support the cost estimates of each department’s budget proposal using the format prescribed by the GPPB and properly fill up the required information thereof, and as may be necessary, designate personnel to be in charge in the preparation of said PPMP;
Require the BAC Secretariat to consolidate PPMP into an indicative APP; and
Direct the BAC to ascertain that all items to be procured are within the APP and to recommend the appropriate modality of procurement to the HoPE.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the BAC to procure common-use supplies from the Procurement Services;
Post all procurement opportunities in the PhilGEPS bulletin board; and
Stop the practice of procuring supplies on a piecemeal basis and adopt public bidding as the primary mode of procurement in compliance with RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to submit to COA a written justification as to why the splitting of procurements was practiced with evidence showing that the prices are advantageous to the government.
Further, we require the BAC Secretariat to consolidate PPMPs and/or purchase requests for each quarter which has the same items, the total amount thereof would serve as the basis for the mode of procurement to be stipulated in the Annual Procurement Plan, and strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 26 of COA Circular No. 1992-386 dated October 20, 1992, and Section 54.1 of Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended the following:
For the Local Chief Executive
Require MPDC and Municipal Engineer to specify the details of the identified priority projects to be charged under the 20% Development Fund including project location, specific implementation schedule, and physical target to avoid generic or lumpsum projects; and
To stop the practice of implementing generic or lumpsum projects without further authority from the Sanggunian to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
For the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer
Require the Barangay to submit their proposal with complete details before the completion of the Annual Investment Plan; and
Initiate the preparation of necessary documents to support cost estimate of projects funded under the 20% Development Plan before the budget preparation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Meticulously and judiciously plan the procurement process of the Municipality;
Ensure that revisions in the original approved Annual Procurement Plan (APP) are supported by BAC Resolution, justifying and recommending the changes, to be approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity; and
Adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR in all stages of the procurement of goods and services and infrastructure projects to achieve transparency, efficiency, and economy in the procurement process.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the accountable person to submit a justification regarding the payment of penalties charged to the disaster fund, explaining why it should not be disallowed in audit; and
Direct the MDRRMO to refrain from using the DRRMF for programs/projects/activities (PPAs) that are not directly related to pre-disaster preparedness, mitigation, response, and rescue operations to ensure that it is utilized solely for approved DRRM-related PPAs.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRMO to:
Prepare and submit supplemental LDRRMFIP for all programs, projects and activities (PPAs) to be charged to the LDRRMF which were not yet included in the annual budget.
Submit the LDRRMFIP to the Municipal Development Council for incorporation in the AIP and for approval of the Sangguniang Bayan; and
Prepare a complete and comprehensive annual LDRRMF Investment Plan and ensure that charges from the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF be included in the current year’s MDRRMFIP, duly approved by the Sangguaniang Bayan Council and Municipal Mayor, in the ensuing year.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare journal entries to record the parcels of land, that were acquired by the Municipality through donations, using their fair market values in order to reflect the correct balance of Land account in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adjust the abovementioned erroneous entries by debiting Due from LGU account and crediting Subsidy from Other LGU account amounting to ₱1,320,000.00 in calendar year 2024. Moreover, furnish the Office of the Auditor with a copy of the MOA to review the terms and conditions agreed upon by both parties.

We recommended that the Local Finance Committee comply with the posting and reporting requirements prescribed in the Local Budget Circular No. 146. Likewise, submit physical copy of said reports to the Office of the Auditor for further evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Accountant refrain from charging expenses not included in the AIP, as well as expenses included in the AIP but not categorized as developmental projects, in accordance with the aforementioned Circular. This is to achieve the purpose of the fund to finance priority development projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain separate subsidiary ledgers, categorized by year of transfer, of all the agency’s unutilized DRRMF balances and not only for those transfers with substantial amount.

We recommended that each Revenue Collector regularly prepare and submit monthly Reports of Accountable Forms (RAAF) for all accountable forms, as stipulated in the aforementioned provisions, in order to timely verify the actual accountabilities of the collectors pertaining to the accountable forms.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive expedite the request for authority to write-off dormant unliquidated cash advances as provided in Section 8 of COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 with the assistance of the Designated Municipal Accountant in the compliance of the supporting documents. Moreover, strictly adhere to the applicable laws, rules and regulations in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive implement the procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances in order to address the issues that are preventing the reconciliation of records between the concerned offices and to establish accurate balances of the PPE accounts that are to be presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor coordinate their efforts in the conduct of regular and massive land tax mapping to cleanse and update their records and come up with accurate list of RPT/SET collectibles. Further, the Municipal Treasurer is required to intensify the collection of delinquent real property taxes that have been long outstanding.

Observe the proper accounting of borrowing costs in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) No. 5; and 

Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the above noted deficiency.

The Acting Municipal Accountant discontinue the loan payments through salary deductions in favor of the private financial institutions for those with minimum net take home pay below P5,000.00, so as to strictly observe the implementation of the minimum take home pay required under Section 48 of the General Provisions of FY 2018 GAA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to the Local Chief Executive to:

Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to strictly adhere with the appropriate charging and obligation of financial transactions, particularly the compensation for other professional services in accordance with the terms of the approved Appropriations of the Municipality and the pertinent provisions of PD 1445 and Volume III of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM); and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to charge the said amounts to the Other Professional Services account under Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE).

We recommended the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and other concerned personnel to implement strict observance of the reglementary period in the submission of financial reports and accounts to the Auditor so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive file the request for authority to write-off dormant unliquidated cash advances to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/ or Supervising Auditor (SA) by complying the procedures in the write-off of dormant accounts provided for in Section 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and Sangguniang Bayan members prioritize the creation of the three (3) statutory position of the LDRRM Office in-charge of administration and training, research and planning, and operations and warning as required by Section 12.b of the RA 10121 to assure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.

Recommendation
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Require the OIC – Municipal Agriculture Officer to prepare and submit the Inventory of the Biological Assets as of December 31, 2021;
Direct the OIC – Municipal Agriculture Officer to furnish the OIC - Municipal Accountant with documents relative to additions, distribution, reproduction, and death of the breeding stock and also render a report which includes the fair market value of each stock, for proper recording in the book of accounts; and
Direct the OIC - Municipal Accountant to provide proper disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements in consonance with IPSAS 27 with information that may affect the fairness of the presentation of the financial statements.

Recommendation
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Require the OIC - Municipal Accountant to reclassify the account to Welfare Goods for Distribution or to inventory accounts using the Perpetual Inventory Method.
Instruct the General Services Officer to prepare the appropriate system in the withdrawal and issuance of the goods such as the following:
Oblige the requesting personnel to prepare the RIS for every withdrawal and/or issuance of goods procured; and
Require the concerned office to prepare and submit SSMI supported by RIS to the GSO, and the GSO will then consolidate all SSMIs and submit it to the accounting office for recording purposes.

Recommendation
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, particularly on the submission to the Office of the Team Leader the required copy of contracts with all its supporting documents within the prescribed period for timely review and evaluation, and to submit requests for inspection of completed projects so that timely inspection will be conducted by the COA Technical Services Office.

Recommendation
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to comply with the existing regulations for the creation and filling up of plantilla positions required to perform the functions of administration and training, and research and planning in accordance with Republic Act 10121, otherwise known as the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (PDRRM) Act of 2010 and in the NDRRMC/DILG/DBM/CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

Recommendation
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to provide the Cashier with a properly enclosed and secured space to ensure that funds and documents in his custody are properly secured and protected from risks of possible losses and intrusion by unauthorized persons.

Recommendation
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Submit valid justification for submitting only the signed payroll to support the P1,704,550.00 disbursements for the TUPAD Program implemented by the LGU;
Submit the required supporting documents per DOLE D.O. 219 series of 2020 and COA such as valid IDs, TUPAD IDs, contract of service, certification from the LGU that the beneficiaries have performed the work under the TUPAD Program, certification that individuals noted to have almost the same data are not from the same family, Memorandum of Agreement, SB Resolution authorizing the LCE to enter into MOA to avail DOLE Programs, certification from the Accountant of no unliquidated cash advances and/or grants from DOLE, and all other necessary documentations; and
Strictly ensure that the transactions of the LGU are supported with complete documentation that would ascertain its propriety and validity.

Recommendation
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Require the Municipal Health Officer and/or Municipal Treasurer to submit the lacking supporting documents/detailed payrolls/records/orders to the Audit Team for payments of SRA and Hazard Pay and accordingly observe compliance on the documentary requirements for these kinds of personnel incentives; and
Require the OIC – Municipal Accountant to ensure that all necessary supporting documents are attached to these claims before approving the payments.

Recommendation
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant Designate to approve and process only those disbursement vouchers supported with complete and valid documentation and approvals; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant Designate to submit the above-mentioned reports within the prescribed timeframe so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.

We recommended that Management require the Head of the Inventory
Committee and the Municipal Accountant to submit the Report on the
Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment as provided in the
manual. Further, disclose the total Road Network System in the Notes to
the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant facilitate the
maintenance of subsidiary ledgers and submit the same with the trial
balance and financial statements for all special accounts of the LGU as
part of the schedules of the General Fund in adherence of NGAS Manual
for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the Due
from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds Accounts to present fairly
the financial position of the municipality.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive file the request for
authority to write-off dormant unliquidated cash advances to the COA
Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/ or Supervising Auditor (SA) by
complying the procedures in the write-off of dormant accounts provided
for in Section 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Management to comply with the deficiencies
noted and ensure that all disbursements of public funds be supported
with complete documents necessary to prove their validity, propriety
and legality.

We recommended that the LGU use the DPRI as the ceiling prices in the
determination of ABC in the procurement of drugs and medicines to
comply with the DOH Administrative Order No. 2019-0040 to achieve
the most advantageous prices.

We recommended that Management direct the Treasurer to return the
excess or unutilized fund from fully implemented projects to its source
agencies and instruct the accountant to prepare and submit to the OP
the Completion Project Report with all the necessary and supporting
documents.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive and Sangguniang Bayan
members prioritize the creation of the three (3) statutory position of the
LDRRM Office in-charge of administration and training, research and
planning, and operations and warning as required by Section 12.b of the
RA No. 10121 to assure implementation of actions and measures related
to disaster risk reduction and management.

We required submission of lacking documentary requirements and
observe strict compliance to the provisions of COA Circular 2012-001
for subsequent land purchases.

We recommended that Management designates a new BAC Secretariat
Head strictly observing the internal control policy as provided in the
Internal Audit Manual for Local Government Units and Republic Act
(RA) 9184.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to re-classify the amount credited to Grants and Donations in Cash account to the Subsidy from National Government account; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for LGUs Volume I in relation to the proper accounting of income, asset, and expense accounts.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit all perfected and awarded contracts together with all the required supporting documents within the prescribed period as mandated under COA Circular No. 2009-001 in order to facilitate timely auditorial and legal review as well as technical review and inspection by the Technical Audit Sector, COA, Regional Office No X.
observations.reason: The BAC still incurred delays in the submission of contracts and lacked appropriate supporting documents as required under pertinent rules and regulations of procurement.

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ascertain that the Disbursement Vouchers are pre-numbered, properly accomplished and adequately supported with documents. The said number should match with that of the Journal Voucher; and

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to attach in each DVs the duplicate/photocopy of the check issued on a particular transaction disbursed through check payment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer to submit the Monthly Status of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations, for both the current and continuing appropriations, in support for the Monthly Pre-Closing Trial Balances, and for the Municipal Accountant to submit a schedule of subsidiary ledger balances of the controlling accounts in the General Ledger in support the trial balances at the end of the quarter.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRM Office and Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No, 2012-002, dated September 12, 2012 relevant to the accrual of the unexpended LDRRMF to a special trust fund in the Trust Fund Books, and to include such unexpended balances in the LDRRMF Investment Plan of the 5% LDRRMF in order to utilize the same to fund LDRRM projects/programs/activities.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials to prepare and submit a duly certified Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF within the period prescribed, which is on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, in compliance with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.reason: The concerned personnel still incurred delays in the submission of monthly reports.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the General Services Officer and Municipal Health Officer to coordinate and come up with a clear defined responsibility for proper inventory-taking, reporting, supervision and disposition of the transferred drugs, medicines, medical supplies and equipment from DOH and PHO.
Require the General Services Officer to inform the Municipal Accountant and furnish her with the receipt of asset for her to recognize it in the books basing on the PTRs issued by the DOH.
The receiving officer should make sure that PTRs are duly accomplished and value of items received are indicated properly otherwise coordinate with the DOH and PHO for that matter.
Distribution and utilization report should be prepared for evaluation and documentation.
Require the Municipal Accountant to record the remaining items on hand in the books of accounts for CY 2020.

Use the proper account codes as prescribed in the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units (RCALGU) in recording and reporting the financial transactions of the agency to have a fair presentation of accounts in the Financial Statements. Also, we recommended that Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses account should not be used in financial transactions involving specific accounts.

Direct the Municipal Accountant reconcile above-mentioned accounts and use the proper account codes as prescribed in the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units (RCALGU) in recording and reporting the financial transactions of the agency to have a fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

Fill up the vacant Plantilla positions and accordingly provide needed appropriation for a more effective administration of those offices and to deliver the basic services needed by the constituents, and

Direct the Human Resource Management Officer to publish the vacancy of positions.

Direct the Human Resource Management Officer to furnish the Accounting Office with a copy of the schedule of earned leave credits of all LGU personnel as of December 31, 2019.
Require the Municipal Accountant to accrue the money value of earned leave credits to be recorded as liability and if no budget or appropriation yet disclose it in the Notes to Financial Statements for information.

We recommended that the Head of General Services Office and the Municipal Accountant maintain Property Cards and Ledger Cards, respectively. Further, direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the Reports on the Physical Count on all PPEs of the LGU and to disclose the total Road Network System in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the General Service Office and the Inventory Committee conduct physical count of Biological Assets and prepare the RPCBA. Likewise, complete the maintenance of Biological Assets Property Cards in consonance with the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that the BAC, through its BAC Secretariat post the required updated APP and awarded contracts and other documents in the GPPB Online Portal for Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act.

We recommended that Management require the suppliers of the foregoing seventeen (17) contracts to submit a valid warranty clause. The same should have to be required in the succeeding purchases charged under BGCMF.
We recommended that Management require the supplier to submit a valid OSS and Income Tax Returns or Business Tax Returns as required by the foregoing provision of GPPB Circular No.01-2020.
We recommended that Management prohibit each Purchasing Department to request goods with reference to brand names. Instead, specifications should have to be the reference.

We recommended that the MSWDO thoroughly review and comply with the requirements provided in the Memorandum of Agreement to ensure that the financial assistance was given to the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Chairperson of the Local School Board and the Municipal Accountant prepare the Income and Expenditures Estimates for year 2020 in compliance with DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that the Inspectorate Team/Committee furnish the Office of the Auditor copies of the IARs within twenty-four (24) hours after the acceptance of all consumable deliveries and/or soon after the inspection and acceptance by the personnel concerned on other items so that timely inspection thereof can be conducted by the Auditors.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive issue an Executive Order or Administrative Order to create a Monitoring and Evaluation Team and direct the GAD Focal Point System to prepare the necessary status and evaluation reports for submission to DILG.

We recommend that Local Chief Executive file the request for authority                            to write-off dormant unliquidated cash advances to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/ or Supervising Auditor (SA) by complying the procedures in the write-off of dormant accounts provided for in Section 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Manage-ment compel the Property Officer, Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant to adhere the provisions of Custodianship and Issuance and Utilization in the Training Handbook on Property and Supply Management System. No. 1445.
